context: permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

context: animal housing producing Meat, dairy cattle (liveweight) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

context: producing Beef cattle, heifer in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what is the functional unit?
title: Functional Unit
context: producing Beef cattle, heifer in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

context: permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

context: producing Beef cattle, cow in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what product is the object of the assessment?
title: Object of Assessment
context: animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

context: producing Beef cattle, bull in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what product is the object of the assessment?
title: Object of Assessment
context: producing Beef cattle, bull in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

context: producing Beef cattle, bull in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what product is the object of the assessment?
title: Object of Assessment
context: producing Beef cattle, bull in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what product is the object of the assessment?
title: Object of Assessment
context: permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

title: Object of Assessment
context: producing Beef cattle, cow in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

question: For this production system, what is the appropriate allocation method? Possible answers are: economic, mass, energy, biophysical, none, none required, system expansion.
title: Allocation Method
context: permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

question: For this production system, what is the functional unit?
title: Functional Unit
context: producing Beef cattle, heifer in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

question: For this production system, what product is the object of the assessment?
title: Object of Assessment
context: producing Beef cattle, heifer in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

Additional description: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.

context: animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. context: animal housing producing Meat, dairy cattle (liveweight) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.

context: producing Beef cattle, cow in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
